forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred near Tasmania will extend a ridge over SA. The ridge will weaken mid-week as a trough moves over the west and north of SA then a cold front will move across the south late Friday and early Saturday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Dry. Morning frost and fog about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Morning fog and patchy frost about the agricultural area. Chance of showers developing about central parts of the pastoral districts, extending into the West Coast district in the evening. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area and and in the far west. Cold at first east of the ranges with areas of frost. Showers about central and western parts of the pastoral districts and the West Coast district. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Morning fog patches about the agricultural area and and in the far west. Cold at first east of the ranges with patchy frost. Showers about the pastoral districts and the West Coast district. Chance of isolated showers over the agricultural area during the afternoon. Possible thunderstorms in the far northeast in the afternoon. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: mostly less than 2 mm about the agricultural area. Totals of 2 to 10 mm about parts of the west and north and isolated falls of 10 to 20 mm are possible.
